title: Resolve merge conflicts for Refactor scheduler.py into package execution root
description:
Merge conflict detected during preflight merge of main into feature/b-f6706ed8.
Conflicted files: .takt/beads/B-09c5359d-corrective.json, .takt/beads/B-09c5359d.json, .takt/beads/B-8024ea9d.json

Auto-merging .takt/beads/B-8024ea9d.json
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .takt/beads/B-8024ea9d.json
Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.

index bb1cf7c,1f75867..0000000
--- a/.takt/beads/B-09c5359d-corrective.json
+++ b/.takt/beads/B-09c5359d-corrective.json

    "title": "Corrective fix for B-09c5359d: Validate scheduler package split and full test suite",
    "agent_type": "developer",
++<<<<<<< HEAD

 +  "status": "ready",
++=======
+   "description": "Blocked reason: 26 failing tests: 7 in test_scheduler_followups.py (methods moved to FollowupManager not forwarded through Scheduler), 14 in test_config_wiring_phase3.py (same + scheduler.py path hardcoded + lease_timeout_minutes missing), 5 in test_model_override.py (_create_followups relocated). Full suite: 26 failed, 956 passed.\n\nRemaining work: 26 failing tests need developer fixes: (1) test_scheduler_followups.py (7 tests): update `scheduler._populate_shared_followup_touched_files()`, `scheduler._planner_owned_followup()`, `scheduler._existing_followups_for()` calls to use `scheduler._executor._followups.*` delegation path; (2) test_config_wiring_phase3.py (14 tests): fix `scheduler.lease_timeout_minutes` access, `scheduler._create_corrective_bead()`, `scheduler._create_followups()` calls, and update FileNotFoundError tests that look for `scheduler.py` to look for `scheduler/__init__.py`; (3) test_model_override.py (5 tests): fix `scheduler._create_followups()` calls.",
+   "status": "done",
++>>>>>>> main
    "bead_type": "task",
    "parent_id": "B-09c5359d",
    "dependencies": [],
@@@ -20,96 -20,303 +25,391 @@@
    "execution_branch_name": "feature/b-f6706ed8",
    "execution_worktree_path": "/Users/oscar.renalias/Projects/codex-agent-orchestration/.takt/worktrees/B-f6706ed8",
    "expected_files": [

handoff_summary.completed: Attempted merge of main into feature/b-f6706ed8, identified conflicts in 3 bead JSON files, resolved all by accepting main's authoritative 'done' states, staged and committed the merge.
handoff_summary.risks: None - pure bead metadata conflict resolved by taking the authoritative completed state from main.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry takt merge for feature/b-f6706ed8 — main has been merged in and conflicts resolved.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — bead state conflicts resolved. Feature branch is now ahead of main with the merge commit.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: All three conflicted files were bead metadata JSON. The feature branch had intermediate states (status: ready/blocked) while main had the completed state (status: done). Resolution: accept main's version unconditionally for all three files. Main is authoritative for bead completion state — the feature branch's versions were pre-completion snapshots that became stale as main progressed.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: N/A — no executable code was changed, only bead JSON metadata files.

conflict_risks: None — bead state conflicts resolved. Feature branch is now ahead of main with the merge commit.

execution_history.summary: Resolved merge conflicts in three bead JSON files by merging main into feature/b-f6706ed8. Accepted main's versions of all three conflicted files since they represent the completed state: B-09c5359d-corrective (done), B-09c5359d (done), B-8024ea9d (done) vs. the feature branch's intermediate states (ready/blocked).

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
